taxonomy: Maxburretia rupicola is a species of flowering plant in the family Arecaceae, commonly known as palms. It belongs to the genus Maxburretia, which is a monotypic genus, meaning it contains only one species. Maxburretia rupicola is native to Peninsular Malaysia, specifically in the states of Pahang and Terengganu. The plant is a type of palm with a unique growth habit, characterized by its solitary stem and crownshaft. Maxburretia rupicola is considered to be an endemic species, meaning it is found only in its native region.
